testcases.input: nums = [2,4,0,9,6] Output: [9,6,6,-1,-1] Explanation: 0th index: 4 is the first integer greater than 2, and 9 is the second integer greater than 2, to the right of 2. 1st index: 9 is the first, and 6 is the second integer greater than 4, to the right of 4. 2nd index: 9 is the first, and 6 is the second integer greater than 0, to the right of 0. 3rd index: There is no integer greater than 9 to its right, so the second greater integer is considered to be -1. 4th index: There is no integer greater than 6 to its right, so the second greater integer is considered to be -1. Thus, we return [9,6,6,-1,-1].

Thus, we return [9,6,6,-1,-1].
